mission.title: Install and Configure llm-d
mission.description: llm-d is a high-performance distributed inference serving stack optimized for Kubernetes, designed to achieve state-of-the-art inference performance with modern accelerators. It is ideal for production deployments requiring efficient orchestration of large language models across various hardware accelerators. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for specific infrastructure resources.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the llm-d command-line interface, which is necessary for managing the llm-d deployment.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d

```

> This command downloads the CLI binary, makes it executable, and moves it to the local bin directory.
commands: curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count in the desired region.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create llm-d-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> This command creates a GKE cluster named 'llm-d-cluster' with 3 nodes.

steps.title: Set Up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to connect to the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region us-central1

```

> This command updates kubeconfig to use the new cluster.
commands: gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region us-central1

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks that the nodes in the cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> This command lists all nodes in the cluster and their statuses.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step sets up autoscaling and monitoring for the llm-d deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/autoscaling.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> This command applies the autoscaling configuration.
> This command applies the monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall llm-d
uninstall.description:
This step removes the llm-d deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss and orphaned resources such as load balancers and persistent volumes.

```bash

kubectl delete deployment llm-d-deployment

gcloud container clusters delete llm-d-cluster --region us-central1 --quiet

```

> This command deletes the llm-d deployment.
> This command deletes the entire cluster and its resources.
commands: gcloud container clusters delete llm-d-cluster --region us-central1 --quiet

upgrade.title: Upgrade llm-d
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data. The upgrade process involves draining nodes, upgrading the deployment, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

kubectl set image deployment/llm-d-deployment llm-d=ghcr.io/llm-d/llm-d:v0.5.1

kubectl get pods

```

> Replace <node-name> with the name of the node to drain.
> This command upgrades the llm-d deployment to the latest version.
> This command verifies that the pods are running the new version.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'ImagePullBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image cannot be found or accessed.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure it is available in the specified registry.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application is crashing due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Review the application logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' and adjust configurations or resource limits.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'NodeNotReady'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** One or more nodes in the cluster are not ready.

**Fix:**
Check the node status with 'kubectl get nodes' and troubleshoot any underlying issues.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster does not have enough CPU resources to schedule the pods.

**Fix:**
Scale up the cluster or adjust resource requests for the pods.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for dynamic multi-workload autoscaling and improved inference scheduling algorithms.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods' showing the llm-d pods in a 'Running' state.
codeSnippets: curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d
gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region us-central1

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the required tools and resources before proceeding with the installation.
